Consider how the number of set bits in 'i' relates to the number of set bits in 'i/2'.
If 'i' is even, 'i' results from shifting 'i/2' to the left. The number of 1s in 'i' and 'i/2' is the same.
If 'i' is odd, 'i' has one more '1' than 'i/2'.
This is a dynamic programming problem where each state depends on a previously computed state.
